description: '''Bećarac''' is a [[humor]]ous form of [[Folk music|folk]] [[song]], originally from rural [[Slavonia]], [[Croatia]], and eventually spreading to southern [[Hungary]] and the [[Vojvodina]] region of [[Serbia]]. Its name comes from the Serbo-Croatian word ''bećar'' (from {{langx|tr|bekâr}}), meaning "bachelor", "reveler" or "carouser". Bećarci verses are typically teasing, mocking and/or lascivious in nature, and are often sung by male company at village parties ("sijelo" or "kirvaj" ([[kermesse (festival)|kermesse]])) or weddings. However, they are also sung by women in equal footing, especially in kolo dance circles.
